name: Abyssal Banshee
description: A demonic looking humanoid that once sang songs of life and love, now twisted by shadows into a screaming banshee.

features.name: Whispy Movement
features.description: The Banshee levitates several feet off the ground. Additionally, attacks made from Far range have disadvantage.

features.name: Focused Cry
features.description: When the Banshee makes a standard attack, make an attack against all targets in a line within Far range. All targets it succeeds against after the first take half damage.

features.name: Nightmarish Reverberations
features.description: Mark a Stressto let out a baleful screech. All targets within Close range must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail lose a Hope and becomeVulnerableuntil spotlighted.

name: Aethix
description: A lifeform of swirling mist formed from aether.

features.name: Arcane Form
features.description: Attacks that deal magic damage to the Aethix trigger a wild magic effect (Introduction of this book). Relentless (2): The Aethix can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Aether Blast
features.description: Spend a Fear to release a beam of arcane energy. All targets in a line up to Far range must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d20+2 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Animate Corpse
features.description: Mark a HP to expend part of the Aethix’s life force and create life. Summon a Perfected Zombie (SRD) from a corpse within Far range, who immediately takes the spotlight.

features.name: Disperse and Reform
features.description: When the Aethix makes a successful standard attack, they can move up to Close range. You can mark a Stress to sweep up the target of the attack and move them with the Aethix.
features.trigger: When the Aethix makes a successful standard attack

features.name: Replenish Vitality
features.description: Countdown (Loop 6). When the Aethix has marked 4 or more of their HP, activate the countdown. When it triggers, if no other creature has cleared HP during the countdown, the Aethix clears 1d4 HP by restoring vitality from the surrounding aether.
features.trigger: Countdown (Loop 6)

name: Animated Colossus
description: A huge statue bestowed with limited sentience.

features.name: Immovable Object
features.description: An attack that would move the Statue moves them two fewer range (for example. Far becomes Very Close).

features.name: Rubble Toss
features.description: Mark a Stress to grab loose rubble and throw it at a point within Far range. If no item is available, mark a HP to tear off a piece of the Statue. All targets within Very Close range of that point must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 2d20+5 physical damage.

features.name: Destroy Terrain
features.description: Spend a Fear to collapse a structure such as a building. All affected targets must mark a Stress and make an Agility Reaction Roll (17) to avoid being crushed. Targets who fail take 4d6+10 direct physical damage.

features.name: Daze
features.description: When the target of the Statue's attack marks HP, they are Dazed. While Dazed, they have disadvantage on Agility Rolls until they clear at least 1 HP.
features.trigger: When the target of the Statue's attack marks HP

name: Arch-Necromancer
description: Un mage en décomposition paré de robes sombres et déchirées.
motives: Fuir pour combattre un autre jour

features.name: Dance of Death
features.description: Placez sous le projecteur 1d4 alliés. Les attaques qu’ils effectuent ainsi infligent la moitié des dégâts, ou la totalité si vous dépensez une Peur.

features.name: Beam of Decay
features.description: Forcez toutes les cibles à Portée Loin à faire un jet de Réaction de Force. Les cibles qui échouent subissent 2d20+12 dégâts magiques et vous gagnez une Peur. Les cibles qui réussissent subissent la moitié des dégâts. Une cible qui coche 2 PV ou plus doit aussi cocher 2 Stress et devient Vulnérable jusqu’à ce qu’elle obtienne Espoir.

features.name: Open the Gates of Death
features.description: Invoquez une Légion de Zombies, qui apparaît à Portée Proche et passe immédiatement sous le projecteur.

features.name: Not Today, My Dears
features.description: Quand le Nécromancien a coché 7 PV ou plus, téléportez-le dans un lieu sûr. Un PJ qui réussit un jet d’Instinct peut remonter la trace de la magie de téléportation jusqu’à sa destination.
features.trigger: Quand le nécromancien a coché 7 pv ou plus, téléportez-le dans un lieu sûr

features.name: Your Life Is Mine
features.description: Compte à rebours (Boucle 2d6). Quand le Nécromancien a coché 6 PV ou plus, activez le compte à rebours. Quand il se déclenche, infligez 2d10+6 dégâts magiques directs à une cible à Portée Proche. Le Nécromancien retire alors des PV ou du Stress égal au nombre de PV cochés par la cible.
features.trigger: Quand le nécromancien a coché 6 pv ou plus, activez le compte à rebours

name: Archangel
description: A powerful and imposing lieutenant of a God, made from solid tight. Motives & Tactics: Protect Hallows Above, dispatch angels, lay down law

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Archangel gains a +3 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Solidify Light
features.description: Mark a Stress to create an impenetrable wall of thin, solid light between two points within Far range with 3 HP and thresholds of 20/40. Attacks automatically succeed against the wall. You can choose the orientation of this wall but it must be linear, extending from a surface, and can't pass through other solid objects.

features.name: Create Portal
features.description: Mark 2 Stress to open a portal within Close range to a known location in any realm. Once per scene, you can use this action to summon two Angels.

features.name: Heavenly Fire
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to unleash apocalyptic fireballs upon a point within Far range. All creatures within Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d20 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Dazzling Light
features.description: When the Archangel takes Major or greater damage, they release a flash of bright light. All creatures within Very Close range must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become blinded, making them Vulnerable until they next act.
features.trigger: When the Archangel takes Major or greater damage

features.name: Smite
features.description: When the Archangel makes a successful standard attack, you can spend a Fear to smite the target, dealing an additional 1d2 magic damage.
features.trigger: When the Archangel makes a successful standard attack

name: Archdruid
description: An expert druid skilled in nature magic.

features.name: Voice of Nature
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight 1d4 allies. While spotlighted in this way, their attacks deal half damage.

features.name: Beastform
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to turn into an animal adversary and immediately take the spotlight in that form. Use the new stat block instead of this one, reverting back when the Archdruid marks their last HP in that form or takes Severe damage.

features.name: Awaken Trees
features.description: Mark 2 Stress to awaken two OakTreants (SRD) from trees within Far range.

features.name: Nature's Fury
features.description: Spend a Fear to create a tempest. All targets within Far range must make an Agility Reaction Roll (17). Targets who fail take 2d20+8 magic damage and become temporarily Vulnerable.

features.name: Entangle
features.description: When the Archdruid makes a successful standard attack, you can mark a Stress to Restrain the target in vines. Archmage Archmages are expert wizards, masters of scholarly study of magic. In addition to casting powerful spells in combat, Archmages can detect and identify obscure magic, scry and teleport, and weave magic to shape surrounding objects and the environment.
features.trigger: When the Archdruid makes a successful standard attack

name: Archer Company
description: A group of soldiers armed with longbows.

features.name: Army
features.description: The Company makes their standard attack against a single Horde target or against all non-Horde targets within Very Close area of a point in range. Horde (2d8+5): When the Company have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d8+5 physical damage instead.

features.name: Split Fire
features.description: Make a standard attack against two targets within Far range. On a success, deal half damage.

features.name: Rain Arrows
features.description: When you make an attack against a compact horde (such as an Infantry Unit or Mage Circle), you gain a +20 bonus to the damage dealt.
features.trigger: When you make an attack against a compact horde (such as an Infantry Unit or Mage Circle)

name: Archmage
description: An expert wizard skilled in arcane magic.

features.name: Magical Wards
features.description: The Archmage is resistant to magic damage.

features.name: Portal
features.description: Mark a Stress to create two portals within Far range, or move an existing portal. The portals remain until the Archmage is defeated.

features.name: Wall of Flame
features.description: Spend a Fear to create a wall of flame between two points within Far range that lasts until the Archmage takes Severe damage or the end of the scene. All creatures in its path must choose a side to be on, and any creature that moves through the wall takes 4d10+3 magic damage.

features.name: Intensify
features.description: When you succeed on a standard attack against a target, you can mark a Stress to also deal damage to a second target the attack succeeds against behind the first and within Far range.
features.trigger: When you succeed on a standard attack against a target

features.name: Redirect
features.description: When the Archmage is targeted by an attack from Close range or further and has used their "Portal" action, you can mark a Stress to move an open portal into the attack's path. When you do so, the attack travels through this portal and out of the other. If a new target is in the attack's trajectory the target takes half damage from the attack. Archpriest Archpriests are expert seraphs, one of their deity’s most skilled fighters and healers. Commando A Commando is an expert ranger. Having spent much time in the wilds, the Commando is friendlier with their beast companion than other humanoids.
features.trigger: When the Archmage is targeted by an attack from Close range or further and has used their "Portal" action

name: Archpriest
description: An expert seraph blessed with divine power.

features.name: Divine Intervention
features.description: Mark all remaining Stress and gain that number of Fear.

features.name: Radiant Beam
features.description: Spend a Fear to emit bright light in a line up to Far range. All targets affected must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become blinded, making them Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Mass Healing
features.description: Spend a number of Fear and clear twice that number of HP for allies within Far range.

features.name: Aura of Protection
features.description: When an ally within Very Close range takes damage, you can mark a Stress to halve the damage. First Knight The First Knight is an expert guardian. They wield an oversized weapon without difficulty and are the foremost guardian of lords and monarchs. General Generals are expert warriors and tacticians who command large armies into battle.
features.trigger: When an ally within Very Close range takes damage

name: Archwizard
description: A veteran spellcaster commanding the most powerful magics.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Archwizard can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Arcane Slip
features.description: Archmage may teleport up to a Far range as part of their action.

features.name: Decoys
features.description: Mark a Stressto create 2 duplicates of the Archwizard within Close range, disguising which one is real. The duplicates have the same difficulty as the Archwizard, but cannot use any of the archwizard’s action or reaction features. When a duplicate suffers damage, it disappears. Archmage may not have more than 2 duplicates active at once.

features.name: Rain of Fire
features.description: Mark 2 Stressand pick a point within Far range. All targets within Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll or suffer4d20magic damage. If more than two targets fail, gain a Fear.

features.name: Casual Dismissal
features.description: When a spell or feature from a class or subclass with a Spellcast Trait is used by a target within Far, force the caster to make a Spellcast Reaction Roll. On a failure, spend a Fearto force the target tomark 2 Stressor cancel the spell with no effect.
features.trigger: When a spell or feature from a class or subclass with a Spellcast Trait is used by a target within Far

name: Ascended: Omniscient Dictator
description: A deity stripped of their immortality.

features.name: Domain Illusion
features.description: Mark a Stress to create an illusion covering the battlefield associated with the Omniscient Dictator's domain, populated with illusory figures. Targets have disadvantage on ranged attacks made through the illusion.

features.name: Spectral Warriors
features.description: When the Omniscient Dictator has an active Domain Illusion, spend a Fear to solidify the illusions, forming 10 Hallowed Soldiers (SRD). The remaining illusion fades.

features.name: Ascended's Blessing
features.description: Either spend a Fear to grant a worshipper a permanent +2 bonus on attack rolls and an additional damage die when rolling damage, or spend 2 Fear to make a worshipper the Omniscient Dictator's Chosen, giving them the High Seraph (SRD) or Chosen Inquisitor stat block if they are Tier 4, or the Chosen Transformed stat block otherwise.

features.name: Create Aspect
features.description: Mark a HP to create an Aspect within Close range. The marked HP can't be cleared until the Aspect is defeated.

features.name: No Hope
features.description: When a PC rolls with Fear while the Domain Illusion is active, they lose a Hope.
features.trigger: When a PC rolls with Fear while the Domain Illusion is active

features.name: i Will Destroy You (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Omniscient Dictator marks their last HP, replace them with the Vengeful Tyrant and immediately spotlight them.
features.trigger: When the Omniscient Dictator marks their last HP

name: Ascended: Vengeful Tyrant
description: A deity, mortal once more and dealt a large blow to their pride. Motives & Tactics: Regain control, conquer world, get revenge

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Vengeful Tyrant makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: You Will Submit
features.description: Targets of the Vengeful Tyrant's attacks who mark an Armor Slot to reduce damage must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Burst of Radiant Light
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, deal 4d8+7 magic damage to the target. All creatures within Very Close range of the target must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or be temporarily blinded, making them Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Instill Fear
features.description: Spend a Fear to terrify all PCs within Far range. They use a d2O as their Fear Die until the end of the scene and must mark a Stress to move closer to the Vengeful Tyrant.

features.name: Words of Power
features.description: If there is no token on this stat block, spend a Fear to begin chanting in an ancient tongue. If there is a token on this stat block, mark a HP to clear the token and finish uttering the words of power that begin to unravel reality. All targets within Far range must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take IdlOO magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage. Aspect A God can semi-permanently siphon off a part of their power to create an Aspect to directly influence affairs. If an Aspect is defeated, it takes decades to centuries for a God to regain the strength to replace them. Demigod A Demigod is the child of a God postAscension. The Demigod inherits some of their parent’s powers, including a Divine Spark, which they can use to Ascend themselves. False God A False God is a mortal with a cult following of believers. The strength of faith of their followers bestows the False God with limited divine powers.

name: Ashen Demon King
description: A hulking being of molten lava and volcanic rock who rules the Circle of the Burned.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Demon King can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Requiem For The Fallen
features.description: If an ally marks its last HP within Close range of the Demon King, you gain a Fear.

features.name: No Rest For The Wicked
features.description: Spend 2 Fearto return up to1d4+1defeated Flamereaver allies to the battle at the points where they first appeared (with no marked HP or Stress).

features.name: Declaration of Hate
features.description: Spend a Fearto make a targetHatedin the eyes of the Demon King until the Demon King is defeated. WhileHated, all Demons have advantage on action rolls against the target. If the target makes a death move while beingHated, they use ad10 for their Hope Die if they choose Avoid Death or Risk it All. The Demon King can only make one targetHatedat a time.

features.name: Incineration Beam
features.description: Mark a Stressto issue a burning beam from the Demon King’s chest at any number of targets in a line up to Far range. All targets must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take4d6 direct magic damage. If the target marks HP, they becomeInflamed.Inflamedtargets mark 1 HP and 1 Stress when they make an action roll and then clear the condition.

features.name: Sovereign Authority
features.description: Spotlight 2 allies within Close range of the Demon King. If they attack aHated target, you canmark a Stressfor each successful attack to add a bonus +10 to their damage rolls. The Demon King can only use this feature once per GM turn.

features.name: Call the Fires Below
features.description: Countdown (4). When the Demon King is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, choose 3 points within Far range. A Very Close area around these points erupts into flames. All targets within these areas must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take4d6+6 magic damage. Additionally, summon a Flamereaver in each area.
features.trigger: Countdown (4)

name: Aspect
description: A mortal manifestation of a God.

features.name: Domain Vision
features.description: Grant a vision to a target the Aspect knows. The target must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become entranced by the vision until they mark 2 Stress or succeed on an Instinct Roll to end it.

features.name: Domain Power
features.description: Mark 3 Stress to use a Tier 1 God power in their domain that doesn't deal damage (such as knowing a target's secrets or crafting a godly weapon) affecting a target within Far range. If a PC is targeted, they can resist the power with a successful Instinct Reaction Roll (20).

features.name: Domain Project
features.description: Long-Term Countdown (6). Activate the countdown when the Aspect's work becomes known. The countdown ticks down each hour (Tier 2 God power) or day (Tier 3 God power) they work on the project.

name: Baba Yaga
description: An ancient witch of the deep woods, Baba Yaga offers terrible truths — and punishes those who ask.

features.name: Flying Mortar
features.description: After making an attack with the Pestle, Baba Yaga can fly anywhere within Far range.

features.name: Grasping Roots
features.description: Spend a Fear to entangle a creature in living roots within Far range and Restrain them until they're freed with a successful Strength Roll. When a creature makes an action roll against the roots, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Withering Word
features.description: Mark a Stress to let Baba Yaga speak a word older than memory. A creature within Close range hears their own death whispered in their native tongue. The target must make a Knowledge Reaction Roll. On a failure, you gain 2 Fear and they take 2d20+4 direct magic damage. On a success, they take half damage and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Summon Chicken-Legged Hut
features.description: Once per day, spend 3 Fear to summon the Chicken-Legged Hut. It appears at Far range.

features.name: The Broom Sweeps Clean
features.description: When Baba Yaga marks HP from an attack within Very Close range, you may mark a Stress to use the Flying Mortar and fly anywhere within Far range. In addition, her broom sweeps away all trace — Baba Yaga becomes Hidden until she is spotlighted again.

name: Cavalry Troop
description: A detachment of mounted soldiers armed with sabers.

features.name: Army
features.description: The Troop makes their standard attack against a single Horde target or against all non-Horde targets within range. Horde (2d8+5): When the Troop have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d8+5 physical damage instead.

features.name: Regroup
features.description: When the Troop fails on an attack roll, they become Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Charge
features.description: Mark a Stress to move to Far range and make a standard attack with advantage against a target. On a success, the target must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Break Through the Ranks
features.description: When you make an attack against a horde equipped to fight at range (such as an Archer Company or Mage Circle), you gain a +20 bonus to the damage dealt.
features.trigger: When you make an attack against a horde equipped to fight at range (such as an Archer Company or Mage Circle)

name: Cephilith Priest
description: A robe-wearing, squid-headed humanoid from the Outer Realms.

features.name: Psychic Blast
features.description: Spend a Fear and choose up to three targets within Close range to make a Knowledge or Instinct Reaction Roll (20). Targets who succeed take 2d10+5 direct magical damage. Targets who fail take 4d10+10 direct magical damage and are Vulnerable until spotlighted.

features.name: Cerebral Suction
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a Vulnerable target within Melee range. The Priest grabs the target and tries to insert their face tentacles into the target's ears, nose, and mouth. The target must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or mark 1d6 Stress. The Priest clears a number of Stress equal to the number of Stress the target marked.

features.name: Telekinetic Grasp
features.description: When the Priest succeeds on a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to Restrain the target until they spend a Hope to clear this condition.
features.trigger: When the Priest succeeds on a standard attack

name: Chaos Dragon
description: A long, thin dragon warped by the chaos realm.

features.name: Wild Magic Skin
features.description: Attacks that deal magic damage to the Dragon trigger a wild magic effect (Introduction of this book).

features.name: Flyby
features.description: While flying, the Dragon moves up to Close range after making a standard attack.

features.name: Constrict
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, mark a Stress and deal 4d6+8 direct physical damage and the target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or become temporarily Restrained by the long tail of the Dragon. If the target is Restrained, the Dragon immediately lifts into the air to Very Far range above the battlefield while holding them.

features.name: Deadly Drop
features.description: While flying, the Dragon can drop a Restrained target they are holding. When dropped, the target is no longer Restrained but starts falling. If the fall isn't prevented during the PC's next action, the target takes 2d20 physical damage when they land.

features.name: Chaos Magic
features.description: Mark a Stress to summon 2 Minor Chaos Elementals (SRD) within Close range. Immediately spotlight both of them.

features.name: Splinter Reality
features.description: Spend a Fear to splinter reality. All creatures must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or split into two copies of themselves, each with their full abilities and half their current HP and Stress (round down). When the Dragon is defeated, all copies merge into one entity again, even if one copy died during combat.

name: Chicken-Legged Hut
description: A living, creaking hut atop giant chicken legs, it stomps through the forest with uncanny grace. Bound to Baba Yaga's will, it shifts the land around it and crushes all who come too close.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Hut can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: It Walks Again
features.description: When the Hut is killed, it reforms at the next dusk. It can only be truly destroyed if the Zlodomovoi within its hearth is slain.

features.name: Threshold of Words
features.description: The Hut can be entered by speaking the right words: A creature within Melee range can make an Instinct or Knowledge Roll (23). If they succeed, they enter the Hut. Baba Yaga knows the words and may enter automatically. While inside, a creature cannot be attacked by the Hut or by any creatures outside. Likewise, they cannot attack anything outside the Hut, including the Hut itself.

features.name: Headless Charge
features.description: Mark a Stress to have the Hut charge in a straight line toward a target within Far range, leaving behind a trail of destruction. On a success, the target takes 4d8+12 physical damage and is knocked back to Close range. Targets within Melee range of the line must make an Agility Reaction roll or take 4d6+8 physical damage and mark a Stress.

features.name: When the House Leaps
features.description: Spend a Fear to let the Hut jump up and slam down, creating a small earthquake. All targets within Close range must make an Agility or Strength Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d10+4 physical damage, and are Vulnerable until they clear a Stress. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Shelter the Witch
features.description: When Baba Yaga is within Close range, mark a Stress to move into Melee and let her enter the Hut as described in Threshold of Words.

name: Chosen Inquisitor
description: A chosen warrior of a deity with an imperious look.

features.name: Divine Flight
features.description: When the Inquisitor is flying, spend a Fear to move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Brand
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 4d6+4 magic damage. If the target marks HP, you can mark one or more Stress to brand the target with the symbol of the Inquisitor's deity, which fades after a long rest. Choose a number of the following options equal to the number of Stress marked that take effect while the brand is active: •	The Inquisitor can discern if the target tells a lie •	The target's Hope features cost an extra Hope •	The target's thresholds are reduced by 2

features.name: Resurrection
features.description: Spend one or more Fear to attempt to return a deceased target within Melee range to life. Roll that number of d6. On a 6, the adversary is returned to life with all HP and Stress restored. Otherwise, the Inquisitor's resurrection attempt permanently fails.

features.name: Smite
features.description: When the Inquisitor makes a successful attack, you can spend a Fear to smite the target, dealing an additional ldl2 magic damage.
features.trigger: When the Inquisitor makes a successful attack

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Inquisitor makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Chosen Inquisitor Chosen Inquisitors are divine warriors who are ruthless fact-finders for their deity. Chosen Transformed If the Chosen is not strong enough to contain the great power bestowed by their God, they are transformed by it. Their skin roils with excess energy that cannot be contained within their body.
features.trigger: When the Inquisitor makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Cinderwretch
description: A monster on all fours that belches flames and incinerates foes.

features.name: Anti-Air Assault
features.description: Before the Cinderwretch makes an attack against a flying target, roll ad6. On a result of 4 or higher, the target’s Evasion is halved against this attack.

features.name: Hellblister
features.description: Spend a Fearand choose a point within Far range. Make an attack against all targets within Close range of that point. Targets the Cinderwretch succeeds against take2d10magic damage. Targets that it misses mark a Stress.

name: Commando
description: An expert ranger skilled in traversing the wiids.

features.name: Two as One
features.description: When the Commando is spotlighted, you can also spotlight a Tier 3 or lower animal adversary currently under their control.

features.name: See it Coming
features.description: The Commando gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty against attacks from beyond Close range.

features.name: Hold Them Off
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, spend a Fear to also attack up to two additional targets within range that the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Focus
features.description: When you succeed on a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to Focus on the target until they become Hidden or the end of the scene. The Ranger gains a +5 bonus to the damage roll against the target. The Ranger can only Focus on one target at a time.
features.trigger: When you succeed on a standard attack

name: Congregant
description: A masked humanoid partaking in a profaned ritual.

features.name: Dark Resolve
features.description: The Congregant is unaffected by attempts to sway them from their worship.

features.name: Minion (9)
features.description: The Congregant is defeated when they take any damage. For every 9 damage a PC deals to a Congregant, defeat an additional Minion within range the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Death Pact
features.description: If more than 3 Congregants are defeated by an attack within Far range, force the attacker to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target isCurseduntil the end of the scene. WhileCursed, if the target rolls a 10 or higher on their Fear die, they must mark a Stress and then clear the condition.
features.trigger: If more than 3 Congregants are defeated by an attack within Far range

name: Convergence, The City of Portals
description: A sprawling metropolis at the nexus of creation, where influential figures from across the realms gather.

features.name: Impossibilities Abound
features.description: Convergence is a nexus of gods, monsters, aliens, and all manner of dimension-hoppers. PCs have disadvantage on action rolls to influence them unless they have leverage against them or know what the NPC cares about.

features.name: Into the Multiverse
features.description: The city of Convergence has a portal or door to every plane of existence.

features.name: Everything Everywhere All at Once
features.description: This city has the solution to any problem, if you can find it. A PC seeking a particular item with wondrous properties can make an Instinct or Presence Roll to find it in the city's pandimensional marketplace.

features.name: Don't Make Waves
features.description: Countdown (6). Start a Countdown (6) that ticks down whenever a PC does something that upsets the city's delicate political equilibrium. When it triggers, the PCs become Wanted until they clear their names with the authorities. When a Wanted PC rolls with Fear, you can summon 1d4 Temporal Enforcers who appear within Very Close range, immediately take the spotlight, and try to apprehend them.

features.name: An Offer You Can't Refuse
features.description: Spend a Fear to have one of the rival factions present the party with an easy solution to a difficult problem in exchange for their allegiance. If the party refuses, each PC must mark a Stress and they can't return to that faction for help in the future.

name: Demigod
description: A large humanoid, the offspring of a God.

features.name: Divine Shout
features.description: Mark a Stress to release a wave of sonic energy affecting all targets up to Close range in front of the Demigod. Targets must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or become Vulnerable until they next roll with Hope.

features.name: Domain Bombardment
features.description: Spend a Fear to make a domain-based attack. All targets within Far range and not under cover must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d20 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Blood Surge
features.description: Once per scene, clear 2 HP and 2 Stress.

features.name: Minor Miracle
features.description: When the Demigod fails on an attack, you can mark 2 Stress to succeed.
features.trigger: When the Demigod fails on an attack

name: Disfavored
description: A chosen warrior who has fallen out of favor with their deity and has been reshaped into a monstrosity as punishment.

features.name: Pinning Strike
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, you can mark a Stress to pin them to a nearby surface. The pinned target is Restrained until they break free with a successful Finesse or Strength Roll.

features.name: Now I Take From You
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a Restrained PC within Melee range. On a success, deal 4d10 magic damage and a random card in the PC's loadout is placed in their vault and can't be accessed until the end of the scene.

features.name: Hateful Aura
features.description: Spend a Fear to release a surge of hatred affecting all PCs within Far range. They use a d20 as their Fear Die until the end of the scene. Clanks As manufactured mechanical beings, Clanks can take all shapes and sizes. While Clanks carry out their assigned instructions to the letter, they have personalities independent and sometimes completely contradictory to their work. Clank Beast Clank Beasts are mechanical creatures with a beast form. Some like this form, while others grumble about why they could not have been built as a humanoid.

name: Elder Fae Noble
description: A powerful fae being that socializes at the fae court. Motives & Tactics: Influence fae courts, seek new pleasures, create mischief

features.name: Allsight
features.description: The Fae can see through invisibility and illusions. PCs can't become Hidden from the Fae.

features.name: Change Season
features.description: Long-Term Countdown (3). Activate the countdown. When it triggers, the season of the surrounding land changes.

features.name: Enhance/Diminish
features.description: Mark a Stress to increase or decrease a PC's trait by 1d4 until their next long rest. An unwilling PC can resist the effect by succeeding on a reaction roll made with that ability trait.

features.name: Manipulate Emotion
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a target within Far range either calm or aggressive. The target clears or marks 1d4 Stress, respectively.

name: Elder Fae Recluse
description: A powerful fae being with a monstrous appearance, who maintains a domain in the Fae Realm.

features.name: This is My Domain
features.description: The Fae senses creatures entering their realm. Creatures can't become Hidden while within the Fae's realm.

features.name: Polymorph
features.description: Mark a Stress to transform a noncombatant creature into any plant or animal adversary.

features.name: Stretch Realm
features.description: Mark a Stress to stretch out the fabric of reality. For all other creatures, the range of their attacks and movement decrease by a category (for example. Close becomes Very Close). This effect ends when the Fae takes Severe damage.

features.name: Terrain Trap
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d8). When the Fae takes damage for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, at a point anywhere on the battlefield the environment shifts to trap all targets within Very Close range of that point. Targets lose a Hope and must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail become Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Finesse or Strength Roll. As a child I tested the legend of the Elder Fae known as the Tooth Faerie. I placed a tooth under my pillow and made an illusory duplicate of myself, asleep in the bed. Sure enough, this Elder Fae creature soon appeared. Amused by the trickery, she awarded me an additionalgold piece. - Eryn
features.trigger: Countdown (Loop 1d8)

name: Elder Fae Wanderer
description: A powerful fae being that likes to roam across realms.

features.name: Eerie Presence
features.description: When a PC rolls a 14 or lower on a Presence Roll made against the Fae, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Fae Bargain
features.description: PCs can make a binding deal with the Fae, such as offering a precious part of themselves in return for calling the Fae's name to receive aid at a later time. Stop Hitting Yourself!: Mark a Stress to force a target to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target deals their standard attack damage to themselves.

features.name: Invisibility
features.description: Mark a Stress to become Invisible until after the Fae's next attack. While Invisible, the Fae has advantage on attack rolls and attack rolls made against them have disadvantage. Elemental Beasts Frost Bear Elemental Beasts are regular animals infused with or entirely made from elemental energy, allowing them to thrive in dangerous environments. Acid Gator Acid Gators live in bogs and marshes, and have a cloud of acid that permanently surrounds their body. The waters they dwell in turn into corrosive acidic pits.

name: Elder Worm
description: A huge worm with diamond teeth that carves through solid rock.

features.name: Bait
features.description: The Worm can use their tail as bait before combat begins. If PCs are focused only in the direction of the tail then you can use the Ambushed environment (SRD) and make a standard attack.

features.name: Burrower
features.description: The Worm can move unhindered through solid rock, leaving a tunnel in their wake.

name: Elemental Hydra
description: A huge quadrupedal beast with four elemental heads.

features.name: Many-Headed Menace
features.description: The Hydra can have up to four heads, one for air, earth, fire, and water. When the Hydra takes Major or greater damage, they lose a head.

features.name: Relentless (X)
features.description: The Hydra can be spotlighted X times per GM turn, where X is the Hydra's number of heads. Each turn must be a different head. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Waterjet (Water Head)
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the target is knocked back to Far range and becomes temporarily l/l/et.

features.name: Lightning Breath (Air Head)
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against all targets in a line up to Far range. Targets the Hydra succeeds against take 3d10+3 magic damage. If the target is l/l/et, they take double damage and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Fire Breath (Fire Head)
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against all targets within Very Close range in front of the Hydra. Targets the Hydra succeeds against take 4d6+8 magic damage, l/l/et targets take half damage and are no longer l/l/et.

features.name: Petrifying Gaze (Earth Head)
features.description: Spend a Fear to force a target within Close range to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, they begin to turn to stone, marking a HP and starting a Petrification Countdown (4). This countdown ticks down on a roll with Fear. When it triggers, the target must make a death move. If the Hydra is defeated, all petrification countdowns end.

features.name: Regeneration
features.description: If the Hydra has less than four heads, spend a Fear to clear a HP and a Stress and regrow a head. Elves Elves are graceful and noble beings. The longevity of Elves means upward mobility in Elven society is severely hindered except through their connections, leading to a cutthroat caste system. Younger Elves advance by challenging others to duels. Duels can result in Elves gaining esteem or the granting of boons from their opponent. Some Elves resort to more underhanded methods to advance, however, such as hiring assassins. Elf Blade-Dancer Younger Elves often train with weapons as the easiest way to challenge elders to duels and progress in society. Elf Spellblade Older Elves dedicate time to becoming proficient in magic in addition to their weapons training. Elf Tycoon Some of the oldest elves have lived long enough to build a business empire. They can crush competition and raise prices in an endless desire for greater riches.

name: Elf Tycoon
description: An elderly elf in charge of a business empire.

features.name: Request Favor
features.description: Call in a favor, such as procuring a rare item, setting up a meeting, or finding out information.

features.name: Blacklist
features.description: Spend a Fear to prevent the PCs from being able to deal with businesses belonging to the Elf.

features.name: Bribe
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to alter a faction's stance on an issue. Faeries Faeries are small creatures of the Fae Realm who have some insectile features such as wings. In Faerie societies Faeries spend their spare time attending balls and special events put on by the noble families of the Fae court. F Faerie Godmother Godmothers are enchanters who can create powerful magic items and animate mundane objects. Faerie Socialite Socialites are fashionable and sociable, with powers reflecting the current season. Faerie Trickster Tricksters use their small size and magical abilities to fight their foes.

name: Extraplanar Army
description: Invading forces from another plane, eager to conquer this world and consume its resources.

features.name: Horde (4d6+2)
features.description: When the Army has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals4d6+2physical instead.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Army can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Inexorable
features.description: When the Army marks 2 or fewer HP from an attack within Melee range, the attacker must mark a Stress.

features.name: World-Eater Locusts
features.description: If no meaningful opposition remains within Very Far, the Army consumes all natural resources in the area. Gain a Fear and the Army clears all Stress and HP.

name: Fallen Shock Troop
description: Une âme maudite liée à la volonté des Déchus.

features.name: Minion (12)
features.description: La Troupe de Choc est vaincue quand elle subit n’importe quels dégâts. Pour chaque tranche de 12 dégâts qu’un PJ inflige à la Troupe de Choc, vainquez un Minion supplémentaire à portée que l’attaque toucherait.

features.name: Aura of Doom
features.description: Quand un PJ coche des PV suite à une attaque de la Troupe de Choc, il perd un Espoir.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Choisissez une cible et placez sous le projecteur toutes les Troupes de Choc Déchues à Portée Proche d’elle. Ces Minions se déplacent à Portée de Mêlée et font un jet d’attaque partagé. En cas de réussite, ils infligent 12 dégâts physiques chacun. Combinez ces dégâts.

name: Fallen Sorcerer
description: Un mage puissant lié par les pactes qu’il a conclus de son vivant.

features.name: Conflagration
features.description: Déchaînez une tempête de feu dévorante et attaquez toutes les cibles à Portée Proche. Les cibles touchées subissent 2d10+6 dégâts magiques directs.

features.name: Nightmare Tableau
features.description: Piégez une cible à Portée Loin dans une puissante illusion de ses pires peurs. La cible est Entravée et Vulnérable jusqu’à ce qu’elle se libère avec un jet d’Instinct réussi.

features.name: Slippery
features.description: Quand le Sorcier subit des dégâts d’une attaque, il peut se téléporter jusqu’à Portée Loin.
features.trigger: Quand le sorcier subit des dégâts d’une attaque

features.name: Shackles of Guilt
features.description: Compte à rebours (Boucle 2d6). Quand le Sorcier est sous le projecteur pour la première fois, activez le compte à rebours. Quand il se déclenche, toutes les cibles à Portée Loin deviennent Vulnérables et doivent cocher un Stress. Une cible peut se libérer avec un jet de Présence ou de Force réussi.
features.trigger: Quand le sorcier est sous le projecteur pour la première fois

name: Fallen Warlord: Realm-Breaker
description: Un guerrier terrifiant qui tranche les mortels comme du papier.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: Le Briseur de Royaumes peut être mis sous le projecteur jusqu’à trois fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour le placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: Quand le Seigneur de Guerre réussit une attaque, tous les PJ à Portée Loin perdent un Espoir et vous gagnez une Peur.

name: Fallen Warlord: Undefeated Champion
description: Ce que seuls les plus redoutés ont une chance de craindre.
motives: Assurer la victoire à tout prix

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: Le Champion Invaincu peut être mis sous le projecteur jusqu’à trois fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour le placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Faltering Armor
features.description: Quand le Champion Invaincu subit des dégâts, réduisez-les de 1d10.

features.name: Shattering Strike
features.description: Faites une attaque standard contre toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche. Les PJ touchés perdent un nombre d’Espoirs égal aux PV cochés suite à cette attaque.

features.name: Endless Legions
features.description: Invoquez un nombre de Troupes de Choc Déchues égal au double du nombre de PJ. Les Troupes de Choc apparaissent à Portée Loin.

features.name: Circle of Defilement
features.description: Compte à rebours (1d8). Quand mis sous le projecteur pour la première fois, activez le compte à rebours. Quand il se déclenche, activez un cercle magique à Portée Loin. Les cibles dans cette zone sont Vulnérables jusqu’à ce qu’elles la quittent. Le cercle peut être retiré en infligeant des dégâts Sévères au Champion Invaincu.
features.trigger: Quand mis sous le projecteur pour la première fois, activez le compte à rebours

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand le Champion Invaincu réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand le champion invaincu réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

features.name: Doombringer
features.description: Quand une cible coche des PV suite à une attaque du Champion Invaincu, tous les PJ à Portée Loin de la cible perdent un Espoir.
features.trigger: Quand une cible coche des pv suite à une attaque du champion invaincu

name: First Knight
description: An expert guardian wielding a huge sword.

features.name: Spinning Strike
features.description: Spend a Fear and make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets you succeed against take 3d12+3 physical damage and are knocked back to Close range.

features.name: Knight's Loyalty
features.description: When the Knight is within Very Close range of an ally who would take damage, you can mark a Stress to move into Melee range of them and take the damage instead.
features.trigger: When the Knight is within Very Close range of an ally who would take damage

features.name: Glancing Blow
features.description: When the Knight takes Major or greater damage, you can mark a Stress to reduce the damage by a category (such as from Severe to Major).
features.trigger: When the Knight takes Major or greater damage

name: Flamereaver
description: A demon with burning red skin and horns made of fire that burst from its forehead.

features.name: Fires of the First Circle
features.description: When the Flamereaver takes magic damage, reduce it by 10. Additionally, when the Flamereaver marks 2 or fewer HP from an attack within Melee range, the attacker must mark a Stress.

features.name: Hellish Provocation
features.description: Make a Standard attack against a target. On a success,mark a StresstoTaunt the target until their next successful attack. The next time theTauntedtarget attacks, they have disadvantage against targets other than the Flamereaver.

features.name: Fiery Rebuke
features.description: When an attack misses the Flamereaver, the attacker must succeed on a Finesse Reaction Roll or take1d10direct magic damage.
features.trigger: When an attack misses the Flamereaver

name: General
description: An expert warrior unmatched with a blade.

features.name: Wear Down the Enemy
features.description: Targets the General makes a successful attack against must mark a Stress.

features.name: Disarming Strike
features.description: Make an attack against a target. On a success, the target is disarmed and their primary weapon is knocked to Very Close range. If the target doesn't have a secondary weapon equipped, they become Vulnerable until they rearm themselves.

features.name: Battle Tactics
features.description: Mark 2 Stress to spotlight all other adversaries within Close range. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Attack of Opportunity
features.description: When a target leaves Melee range of the General, you can make a standard attack against them. Headmaster A Headmaster is an expert sorcerer. As head of a magical school, the Headmaster assists other sorcerers with controlling their innate magic. Mastermind Masterminds are expert rogues, charismatic leaders of a secret guild or underground organization. Virtuoso Virtuosos are expert bards who delight and entertain others with captivating words or music. They are known across the realm for their epic accomplishments, whether true tales or embellished for entertainment.
features.trigger: When a target leaves Melee range of the General

name: God-King
description: A divinely-empowered monarch claiming dominion over the whole world.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The God-King can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the God-King makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Kingsguard
features.description: Spend a Fearto summon a number of Junior Seraphs equal to the number of PCs. They appear at a Close range.

features.name: None Shall Stand in My Way
features.description: Spend 2 Fearto activate God-King and up to2d4allies.

features.name: Majestic Disdain
features.description: When a PC succeeds on an action roil against the God-King, the PC must make a Presence Reaction Roll or mark1d4Stress. On a success, they still mark a Stress.
features.trigger: When a PC succeeds on an action roil against the God-King

name: Greater Fire Demon
description: A sizable demon whose form is wreathed in white hot flames.

features.name: Fires of Spite
features.description: When the Fire Demon takes damage, reduce it by1d12. If the Fire Demon marks 2 or fewer HP from an attack, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: You mustspend a Fearto spotlight the Fire Demon. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Hope Eater
features.description: Spend a Fearto make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Fire Demon succeeds against take4d10+8magic damage and lose a hope for each HP they marked. If a target can’t lose Hope, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Furious Retaliation
features.description: When an ally within Close range marks 2 or more HP from an attack, you canmark a Stressto force the attacker to make an Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, the attacker takes2d10+6magic damage and has disadvantage on its next attack roll. On a success, the target marks a Stress.
features.trigger: When an ally within Close range marks 2 or more HP from an attack

name: Hallowed Archer
description: Des soldats spirituels avec des arcs sanctifiés.

features.name: Punish the Guilty
features.description: L’Archer inflige le double de dégâts aux cibles marquées Coupables par un Haut Séraphin.

features.name: Divine Volley
features.description: Faites une attaque standard contre jusqu’à trois cibles.

name: Hallowed Assassin
description: A divinely-empowered killer dispatched to vanquish the godsʼ most persistent foes.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Assassin can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Pierce Weakness
features.description: The Assassin does not loseHiddenby moving. When the Hallowed Assassin succeeds on an attack where they had advantage, they deal direct damage.

features.name: Cull the Chaff
features.description: Spend 2 Fearto force all targets within Very Close range to make an Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, they mark 3 HP.

features.name: Wreathed in Hallowed Grace
features.description: Mark a Stressto move up to a Far range and become Hidden.

features.name: Peerless Disguise
features.description: When a PC rolls with Fear,spend 2 Fearto reveal that an NPC or adversary in the scene is actually the Assassin. The Assassin immediately takes the spotlight, gaining advantage on their attack unless the target succeeds on an Instinct Reaction Roll.
features.trigger: When a PC rolls with Fear

name: Hallowed Choir
description: A heavenly host of winged humanoids whose song calls forth tears of religious awe.

features.name: Horde (2d8+3)
features.description: When the Choir has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d8+3 magic damage instead.

features.name: Aural Assault
features.description: Spend a Fear to have the Choir unleash divine energy. Remove all tokens from this stat block (see Celestia Coda). For each token removed, deal 1d12 direct magic damage to all enemies within Close range.

features.name: Celestia Coda
features.description: Countdown (Loop 3). The Choir's divine song builds on itself with each refrain. Activate the countdown when the Choir is first spotlighted. It ticks down on every action roll. When it triggers, place a token on this stat block. The Choir gains a bonus to their Difficulty and standard attack damage equal to the number of tokens on this stat block.
features.trigger: When the Choir is first spotlighted

name: Hallowed Soldier
description: Des âmes de fidèles, élevées avec des armes divines.

features.name: Minion (13)
features.description: Le Soldat est vaincu quand il subit n’importe quels dégâts. Pour chaque tranche de 13 dégâts qu’un PJ inflige au Soldat, vainquez un Minion supplémentaire à portée que l’attaque toucherait.

features.name: Divine Flight
features.description: Tant que le Soldat vole, dépensez une Peur pour se déplacer jusqu’à Portée Loin au lieu de Portée Proche avant d’agir.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Choisissez une cible et placez sous le projecteur tous les Soldats Sanctifiés à Portée Proche d’elle. Ces Minions se déplacent à Portée de Mêlée et font un jet d’attaque partagé. En cas de réussite, ils infligent 10 dégâts physiques chacun. Combinez ces dégâts.

name: Headmaster
description: An expert sorcerer teaching others in magic.

features.name: Manipulate Magic
features.description: You can mark a Stress to affect a spell the Headmaster casts in one of the following ways: •	Extend the range by a category, such as from Close to Far. •	Affect an additional target with the Headmaster's standard attack.

features.name: Hush
features.description: Mark a number of Stress to attempt to silence that many targets within Close range of the Headmaster. The targets must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become temporarily Silenced. While Silenced, they are unable to cast spells or speak aloud while within this range.

features.name: Arc Lightning
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, deal 4d10 magic damage and half damage to any additional targets within Very Close range of the initial target that the attack would succeed against.

features.name: Arcane Ward
features.description: Once per scene, when the Headmaster or an ally within Close range takes magic damage, they are resistant to the damage.

name: High Seraph
description: Un champion divin, à la tête d’une armée sacrée de guerriers qui font respecter la volonté de leur dieu.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: Le Séraphin peut être mis sous le projecteur jusqu’à trois fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour le placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Divine Flight
features.description: Tant que le Séraphin vole, dépensez une Peur pour se déplacer jusqu’à Portée Loin au lieu de Portée Proche avant d’agir.

features.name: Judgment
features.description: Rendez une cible à Portée Loin Coupable aux yeux du dieu du Séraphin jusqu’à ce que le Séraphin soit vaincu. Tant que Coupable, la cible ne gagne pas d’Espoir sur un résultat avec Espoir. Le Séraphin inflige des dégâts Sévères aux cibles Coupables sur une attaque standard réussie. Une seule cible à la fois.

features.name: God Rays
features.description: Reflétez un éclat de divinité sous forme d’un rayon de lumière brûlant frappant jusqu’à vingt cibles à Portée Très Loin. Les cibles doivent faire un jet de Réaction de Présence (avec désavantage si Coupables). Les cibles qui échouent subissent 4d6+12 dégâts magiques. Les cibles qui réussissent subissent la moitié des dégâts.

features.name: We Are One
features.description: Une fois par scène, placez sous le projecteur tous les autres adversaires à Portée Loin. Les attaques qu’ils effectuent infligent la moitié des dégâts.

name: Host of Morozhenye
description: Frozen where they begged for mercy, the Morozhenye return as hollow echoes of the condemned. Their limbs crack with ice, their eyes see nothing, and their touch brings silence. They do not speak. They do not feel. They remember only the cold.

features.name: Horde (2d10+2)
features.description: When the Host has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d10+2 magical damage instead.

features.name: Punish the Guilty
features.description: The Host deals double damage to targets marked Guilty by Morozko.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Legion can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Drain the Ember
features.description: When the Host's attack causes a target to mark HP, you can mark a Stress to gain a Fear and force the target to mark a Stress.

name: Infantry Unit
description: A detachment of disciplined soldiers armed with spears and shortswords.

features.name: Army
features.description: The Unit makes their standard attack against a single Horde target or against all non-Horde targets within range. Horde (2d10+5): When the Unit have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2dlO+5 physical damage instead.

features.name: Loss of Formation
features.description: When the Unit moves beyond Close range, they become Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Throw Spears and Draw Swords
features.description: Once per scene, make a standard attack with advantage against a target within Close range. The Unit's standard attack changes to a shortsword (Melee range and same damage). The Unit loses their "Loss of Formation" and "Spear Wall" features and Army foes attacking the Unit (such as an Archer Company or Mage Circle) no longer apply a damage bonus against the Unit.

features.name: Spear Wall
features.description: When you make an attack against a loosely organized horde (such as a Monster Horde or Cavalry Troop), you gain a +20 bonus to the damage dealt.
features.trigger: When you make an attack against a loosely organized horde (such as a Monster Horde or Cavalry Troop)

name: Jaeger Assassin
description: A humanoid creature created for hunting and killing.

features.name: Devastating Strikes
features.description: The Jaeger has a critical success on an attack roll of 19 or 20.

features.name: No Hope
features.description: When a PC rolls with Fear while within Close range of the Jaeger, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Impale
features.description: Make an attack against a target with the Jaeger's tail. On a success, the target takes 4d6+8 physical damage. When a target uses armor to reduce damage from this attack, they must mark 2 Armor Slots.

features.name: Track
features.description: When presented with a PC's scent, activate a Tracking Countdown (6), which ticks down when a PC rolls with Fear, or on any action roll if the PC's location is known. If the PCs are not in the vicinity, make this a long-term countdown instead. When it triggers, the Jaeger catches up to the PCs.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Jaeger makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. them and prevent them from spellcasting.
features.trigger: When the Jaeger makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Jaeger Magehunter
description: A humanoid creature created for hunting magical beings.

features.name: Lethal Hunter
features.description: When a PC equipped to deal magic damage rolls with Fear against the Jaeger, they lose a Hope.

features.name: Track
features.description: When a PC makes a Spellcraft Roll, activate a Tracking Countdown (6), which ticks down when a PC rolls with Fear, or on any action roll if the PC's location is known. If the PCs are not in the vicinity, make this a long-term countdown instead. When it triggers, the Jaeger catches up to the PCs.

features.name: Constrict
features.description: When the Jaeger has grappled a target in a tendril, squeeze the life from the target. The target must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or take 4d12+4 direct physical damage. The Difficulty to escape the grapple increases by 2.

features.name: Grapple
features.description: When the Jaeger succeeds on a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to grapple the target in a tendril. The target is Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Strength or Finesse Roll (16) or the Jaeger takes Major or greater damage. While Restrained in this way the target can't use magic. Jelloon Jelloons are floating jellyfish-like creatures found in enchanted forests and other magical lands. They levitate above the ground, floating like a balloon. Jelloons use their long tendrils to restrain, shock, and poison their prey.
features.trigger: When the Jaeger succeeds on a standard attack

name: Kraken
description: Une bête légendaire des mers, plus grande que le plus grand galion, avec des tentacules à ventouses et une gueule terrifiante.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: Le Kraken peut être mis sous le projecteur jusqu’à trois fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour le placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Many Tentacles
features.description: Tant que le Kraken a 7 PV cochés ou moins, il peut faire son attaque standard contre deux cibles à portée.

features.name: Grapple and Drown
features.description: Faites un jet d’attaque contre une cible à Portée Proche. En cas de réussite, agrippez-la avec un tentacule et entraînez-la sous l’eau. La cible est Entravée et Vulnérable jusqu’à ce qu’elle se libère avec un jet de Force réussi ou que le Kraken subisse des dégâts Majeurs ou supérieurs. Tant qu’Entravée, la cible doit cocher un Stress quand elle fait un jet d’action.

features.name: Boiling Blast
features.description: Crachez une ligne d’eau bouillante sur un nombre quelconque de cibles en ligne jusqu’à Portée Loin. Toutes les cibles doivent réussir un jet de Réaction d’Agilité ou subir 4d6+9 dégâts physiques. Si une cible coche un Emplacement d’armure pour réduire les dégâts, elle doit aussi cocher un Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand le Kraken réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand le kraken réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Leviathan
description: A huge sea serpent with head, body, and tail emerging from the water.

features.name: Multiple Segments
features.description: The Leviathan is composed of a Head, Body, and Tail segment. Move each segment to a new position when the Leviathan moves. If an attack would affect multiple segments of the Leviathan, multiply damage by the number of segments hit. Relentless (3): The Leviathan can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn, once for each segment. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Launch (Head)
features.description: Mark a Stress to launch into the air. You can make a standard attack against an airborne target up to Close range above the water. When the Leviathan reenters the water, all targets in the water up to Close range must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or be pushed to Far range from the resulting tidal wave.

features.name: Drown (Middle)
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, mark a Stress to hold the target beneath the water. The target is Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Strength Roll or the Leviathan takes Major or greater damage. While Restrained and Vulnerable in this way, a target must mark a Stress when they make an action roll.

features.name: Whirlpool (Tail)
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a whirlpool at a point within Close range. All targets in the water within Far range must make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, they must mark a Stress and move up to Close range towards that point.

features.name: Swallow
features.description: When the Leviathan deals Major or greater damage on a standard Bite attack, you can spend a Fear to swallow the target. While swallowed, the creature is Restrained within the Leviathan and must mark a Stress when they act. If the Leviathan takes Major or greater damage, all swallowed creatures are released, taking 1d20+5 physical damage and moving to Very Close range of the Leviathan.
features.trigger: When the Leviathan deals Major or greater damage on a standard Bite attack

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Leviathan makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear. Liches Liches have achieved immortality through a dark ritual separating their soul from their body. They require a regular sacrifice of souls to maintain their extended life. Without it, the body of a Lich continues to age and eventually decays. While the soul of the Lich remains secured in the Mortal Realm, the Lich can return to life if they die. Lich A typical Lich is an arcane scholar or wizard who has stored their soul in an object called a phylactery. After death, the Lich’s body begins to reform from the phylactery after a long rest. Undying Sovereign An Undying Sovereign is a Lich who rules a nation as their God Monarch. The Lich has split their soul into multiple pieces placed within their royal regalia. They have also weakened the soul of their heir such that if the Lich dies they can take over the heir’s body after the coronation. If a different person takes the throne, the regalia corrupts the next monarch over time.
features.trigger: When the Leviathan makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Lich
description: An undead mage who consumes souls to prolong their life.

features.name: Death Aura
features.description: Creatures within Melee range of the Lich must mark a Stress when they make an action roll.

features.name: Reveal Yourself
features.description: Mark a Stress to surround a target within Far range with a powerful illusion showing their life up to this moment. While trapped, the target is Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Presence Roll. If they roll a failure with Fear while Restrained in this way, the Lich learns their True Name.

features.name: Pawn
features.description: Spend a Fear to call out the True Name of a target that the Lich has learned. The target must make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target becomes Controlled by the Lich and must lose a Hope. While Controlled the target must make an Instinct Reaction Roll when they act. On a success, clear the condition. On a failure, they act on the Lich's orders.

features.name: Death Wave
features.description: If there is no token on this stat block, spend a Fear to gather necrotic energy, and place a token on this stat block. If there is a token on this stat block, spend a Fear to clear the token and release a wave of necrotic energy. All targets within Far range must make an Instinct Reaction Roll, taking IdlOO magic damage on a failure, or half damage on a success.

features.name: Counterspell
features.description: Once per scene, mark 2 Stress to negate a spell cast within Close range.

name: Living Nightmare
description: A shifting physical manifestation of terror made of sinew, bone, and shadow.

features.name: Embrace Darkness
features.description: When the Nightmare attacks from dim light or shadow, the target must succeed on an Instinct or Presence Reaction roll. On a failure, the Nightmare learns their fear and adds its Experience to attacks made against the target.

features.name: Pierce Your Soul
features.description: Spend a Fearto make an attack, lashing out with spined tentacles at all targets within Close range. Targets the Nightmare succeeds against take4d12+10physical damage and must mark a Stress.

features.name: Unmitigated Fear
features.description: When the Nightmare deals Major damage to a target, the target also marks a Stress and isTerrified.TerrifiedPCs areVulnerableuntil they roll with Hope or gain Hope from another source. When TerrifiedPCs take damage, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Nightmare deals Major damage to a target

name: Mage Circle
description: A detachment of magic users.

features.name: Army
features.description: The Circle makes their standard attack against a single Horde target or against all non-Horde targets within Very Close area of a point in range. Horde (2d8+5): When the Circle has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d8+5 magic damage instead.

features.name: Warp Battlefield
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose two points within Far range. The terrain between these points is warped and a successful Agility Roll (12) is required to move across this region.

features.name: Teleport
features.description: Mark a Stress to teleport the Circle to a point within Far range.

features.name: Chained Blasts
features.description: When you make an attack against a tightly packed or dim-witted horde (such as an Infantry Unit or Monster Horde), you gain a 4-20 bonus to the damage dealt.
features.trigger: When you make an attack against a tightly packed or dim-witted horde (such as an Infantry Unit or Monster Horde)

name: Mastermind
description: An expert rogue skilled in deception.

features.name: Sneak Attack
features.description: If the Mastermind is Hidden when they make a successful standard attack against a target, they deal 4d10+10 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Master Thief
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, mark a Stress to deal 3d8+4 physical damage and the Mastermind steals a bag of gold or one item or consumable from the target's inventory.

features.name: Living Darkness
features.description: Mark a Stress to summon a Hungering Darkness that surrounds them. The Mastermind becomes Hidden within the Hungering Darkness and is immune to the effects of their ''Envelop" action.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Mastermind makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Mastermind makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Mirrored Soul
description: A shadowed reflection of a PC with access to all the cards in the PC’s Vault.

features.name: I Am Your Potential
features.description: When the Mirrored Soul is created, they gain all the cards in the Vault of the PC they were created from. The cards return to the PC’s Vault once the Mirrored Soul is defeated or the scene ends.

features.name: Your Power is Mine
features.description: Mark Stress(minimum 1) equal to a card’s recall cost to use its ability as if the Mirrored Soul had spent the maximum named resources called for. That card is then locked and cannot be used again.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Mirrored Soul makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Mirrored Soul makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Monster Horde
description: An unruly horde of monsters.

features.name: Army
features.description: The Horde makes their standard attack against a single Horde target or against all non-Horde targets within range. Horde (2d10+5): When the Horde has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2dlO+5 physical damage instead.

features.name: Howl
features.description: Let out a bone-chilling howling noise. All targets within Close range must mark a Stress. If the target's standard attack range is Far or Very Far or they are mounted, they become temporarily Vulnerable.

features.name: Tear To Shreds
features.description: When you make an attack against a Vulnerable target, you gain a +10 bonus to the damage dealt. Avians Avians are bird-like winged humanoids capable of flight. Some live amongst civilization but many prefer to build their homes in the wilds. Since carrying bulky or heavy items would reduce their flight capabilities, Avians have an innate ability to store items within extradimensional spaces. They can use this trick in combat to surprise foes. Avian Aeronaut An Aeronaut is an agile foe skilled with a bow who has a variety of arrow types.
features.trigger: When you make an attack against a Vulnerable target

name: Morozko
description: An ancient spirit of winter and judgement, Morozko rewards humility and punishes pride. His presence brings silence, snow, and the cold certainty that no soul escapes the test of frost.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: Morozko can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Breath of Endless Winter
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to release a frosty breath in front of Morozko within Far range. All targets in this area must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 4d8+7 magic damage and are Restrained by ice until they break free with a successful Strength Roll. Targets who succeed must mark 2 Stress or take half damage. Targets roll all Reaction Rolls against this feature with disadvantage if they are marked Guilty.

features.name: Judgment
features.description: Spend a Fear to make a target Guilty in the eyes of the Morozko until Morozko is defeated. While Guilty, the target doesn't gain Hope on a result with Hope. When Morozko succeeds on a standard attack against a Guilty target, they deal Severe damage instead of their standard damage. Morozko can only mark one target at a time.

features.name: Unleash the Stillborn Frost
features.description: Spend a Fear to summon a Host of Morozhenye, frozen souls who died unrepentant in the snow. They appear at Close range and immediately take the spotlight.

features.name: Frozen in Judgement
features.description: When a PC within Close range is successfully hit by an attack, mark a Stress to cover them in ice and make them Restrained.

name: Ophanim
description: A being of many eyes, affixed to floating rings made of solid light, with a single glowing eye in the center.

features.name: All-Seeing
features.description: The Angel can see through illusions and invisibility. PCs can't become Hidden from the Angel.

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Angel. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Watchful Gaze
features.description: Probe the mind of all targets within Close range. All targets must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, a target must mark 1d4 Stress and the Angel can read a thought or memory.

features.name: Banish
features.description: Mark a Stress to force a target within Far range to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target is temporarily Banished to a different realm. When a PC rolls with Hope, the Banished target can make a new Instinct Reaction Roll with a -1 penalty applied to the Difficulty, ending the banishment on a success.

features.name: Burning Gaze
features.description: When the Angel deals Major or greater damage, the target is Ignited until they're extinguished with a successful Finesse Roll (16). While Ignited, the target takes 1d2O magic damage when they make an action roll.
features.trigger: When the Angel deals Major or greater damage

features.name: Spin Up Wheels
features.description: When the Angel marks HP, you can mark a Stress to gain a +3 bonus to the Angel's standard attack damage.
features.trigger: When the Angel marks HP

name: Oracle of Doom
description: Un immortel imposant et incarnation du destin, maudit à ne voir que les issues funestes.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: Quand l’Oracle réussit une attaque, tous les PJ à Portée Loin perdent un Espoir et vous gagnez une Peur.

features.name: Walls Closing In
features.description: Quand une créature obtient un échec à Portée Très Loin de l’Oracle, elle doit cocher un Stress.

features.name: Pronounce Fate
features.description: Présentez à une cible à Portée Loin une vision de son cauchemar personnel. La cible doit faire un jet de Réaction de Savoir. En cas d’échec, elle perd tout son Espoir et subit 2d20+4 dégâts magiques directs. En cas de réussite, elle subit la moitié des dégâts et perd un Espoir.

features.name: Summon Tormentors
features.description: Une fois par jour, invoquez 2d4 Minions de Tier 2 ou inférieur en rapport avec le cauchemar personnel d’un des PJ. Ils apparaissent à Portée Proche de ce PJ.

features.name: Ominous Knowledge
features.description: Quand l’Oracle voit une créature mortelle, il connaît instantanément l’un de ses cauchemars personnels.
features.trigger: Quand l’oracle voit une créature mortelle

features.name: Vengeful Fate
features.description: Quand l’Oracle coche des PV suite à une attaque à Portée Très Proche, repoussez l’attaquant à Portée Loin et infligez 2d10+4 dégâts physiques.
features.trigger: Quand l’oracle coche des pv suite à une attaque à portée très proche

name: Otherworld Commander
description: A stoic, battle-hardened veteran from another world.

features.name: Relentless (2)
